title: Fun Science Stories: Raccoons and Space Sperm
summary: This article shares cool science stories that almost got missed. It talks about raccoons, which are animals that live near people and like to get into trash. Scientists found that raccoons are good at solving puzzles, like opening boxes to get marshmallows. They keep trying even after they get the treat, showing they are curious and smart. Another story is about sperm in space. Scientists tested sperm from humans, mice, and pigs in a machine that acts like zero gravity. They found that sperm get lost and can't find eggs easily in space, but adding a special chemical called progesterone can help. The article also mentions other stories, like finding old writings and folding crepes, but focuses on these two main ones.

keywords.explanation: animals that are good at solving puzzles and live near people
tiny cells from males that help make babies
the area outside Earth where there is no gravity
games or boxes that need to be opened or solved
a chemical that can help sperm in space

background_read: This article is about science stories that are interesting but almost not shared. It helps kids learn about animals and space in a fun way. Raccoons are common in cities and can be pests, but scientists study them to understand their smart behaviors. Space exploration is exciting, and scientists are learning how living things might survive there, like with sperm and reproduction. The stories show how science helps us discover new things every day.
Article_Structure: WHAT: Stories about raccoons solving puzzles and sperm in space
WHERE: In labs and urban areas
WHY: To share cool science discoveries that almost got missed

perspectives.description: Scientists study raccoons to see how they solve problems and adapt to human environments.
Researchers test sperm in zero gravity to understand challenges for reproduction in space.
